text: Meeting deadlines is a key skill in both academic and professional life. Effective time management, such as breaking a project into smaller tasks, helps you track progress. Prioritization ensures you focus on the most important steps first, reducing last-minute stress. Additionally, scope management is crucial: if the workload becomes too heavy, avoid overcommitting and adjust your goals early. Finally, clear communication with managers or teammates about your progress or potential delays builds trust and allows for realistic adjustments. By practicing these habits, you can meet deadlines calmly and deliver high-quality work.
